entries.name: Lucky
entries.detail: Series premieres July 15 on Apple TV
entries.description: Anya Taylor-Joy reteams with Apple TV after The Gorge earned Perfect B-movie status last year. In this crime-thriller series, AT-J plays a reformed criminal who’s forced to return to her ways (this always happens) after what was supposed to be a seamless multi-million-dollar heist goes sideways. We’re kinda jealous she’s pursued by Annette Bening tbh. AT-V drops the first two episodes on July 15, and Season 1 will wrap on August 19. — Arno

entries.name: The Hawk
entries.description: Will Ferrell returns to your smaller screens in this ensemble comedy series that hauls out the trusty sports comeback story trope and reunites him with his former "SNL" co-stars Molly Shannon and Chris Parnell. Ferrell handled writing duties on his own and stars as Lonnie "The Hawk" Hawkins, a top golfer years past his heyday who wants back inside the winner's circle. Netflix will drop alllllllll 10 episodes on July 16. — Arno

entries.name: The Odyssey
entries.description: Summer movie season doesn’t get more epic than a new Christopher Nolan film based on one of the most well-known stories in history, filmed entirely in 70mm IMAX. Matt Damon leads a stacked cast as the Greek king Odysseus as he journeys home to reunite with his wife, Penelope (Anne Hathaway). This will be the most fantastical story we’ve seen from Nolan, and with an estimated $250 million budget, his cinematic interpretations of things like the Trojan Horse, Greek gods, sirens, and mythical beasts should make for some eye-popping spectacle worthy of a trip to the cinema. — James

entries.name: Evil Dead Burn
entries.description: I’m not sure what to make of this latest entry into Sam Raimi’s seminal story of psychos from beyond the grave. The vibes feel very early 2000s, thanks to the dingy color grading and general Rob Zombie-style hillbilly family dinner scene in the trailer. But that styling might work in Evil Dead Burn’s favor. Things were already heading that direction with the last flick, Evil Dead Rise, which has a decidedly Y2K-goth-girl-goes-crazy energy that could have easily been fan edited into a TikTok scored by Evanescence (extreme complimentary), and that movie was a hit. This time, we'll follow a widow whose visit with her in-laws gets goopy when they all start to transform into deadites. Director Sébastien Vanicek has only one other feature to his name, so my guess is he’s going to come out swinging with this one. Frankly, it kinda sounds groovy. — Adrienne

entries.name: Little House on the Prairie
entries.description: Encountering Laura Ingalls Wilder’s seminal book series about life in the American West has been a formative experience for multiple generations of little girls. Both Gen X and (elder) Millennials even got to see the Ingalls family on the small screen in a TV series that lasted nearly 10 years. Now a whole new generation gets to enjoy the adventures of Laura, Mary, Ma, Pa, and even Nellie in the Netflix series that will adapt the third book in the series (that’s the one that’s actually about the prairie, iykyk) and also expand beyond the family’s journey to include a larger picture of life on the frontier. — Adrienne

entries.name: Moana
entries.description: If you’re not a parent (or a Disney adult), you’d be forgiven for asking how there could possibly be another Moana movie on the way. As The Lion King, Lilo & Stitch, and 2024’s Moana 2 demonstrated, live-action Disney remakes and Moana sequels have the potential for billion-dollar returns at the box office. But after recent live-action misfires (The Little Mermaid, Snow White), what reasons do we have to believe this version will be a hit? For starters, the Moana franchise is still incredibly popular. Last year, Moana 2 was the #2 most streamed movie across all platforms, and the original Moana was at #4 with 5.8 billion minutes watched, according to Nielsen. Unless Dwayne Johnson’s hair negates his box office power (like Samson in reverse), Moana is likely to sail right to the bank.

entries.name: Minions & Monsters
entries.description: The Minions are already an instant win for children everywhere, so we’re going to focus on what you big kids (aka adults) might like to know. The latest Despicable Me spin-off is set in 1920s Hollywood where everyone’s favorite henchmen will set out to find the perfect lead actor for their new monster movie. Franchise mastermind Pierre Coffin returns as both writer-director and the voice of the Minions, and keep an ear out for the A-list voice cast, which includes Jeff Bridges, Allison Janney, Jesse Eisenberg, and Christoph Waltz, among others. — Adrienne

entries.name: Elle
entries.description: This Legally Blonde prequel series gives Elle Woods' teen years a light facelift by relocating the high schooler and future lobbyist from Bel-Air to 1990s grunge-era Seattle. Lexi Minetree plays the young fish out of water, and showrunner Laura Kittrell was a writer/producer on Issa Rae’s “Insecure.” — James

entries.name: X-Men 97
entries.description: Fans have patiently waited two years to find out what will happen after the cliffhanger finale of Season 1, which left several of our mutant heroes lost in time, and in a post-credit sequence, revealed X-Men supervillain Apocalypse lying in wait. The first three (of nine) episodes drop July 1 on Disney+, and good news! The series was already renewed for a third season. — James

entries.name: Enola Holmes 3
entries.description: Enola Holmes is back with a third adventure this month that will take the youngest Holmes from the altar all the way to the cliffs of Malta. Millie Bobby Brown reprises her role as the young detective, who looks to be headed for a life of domestic bliss until her older brother Sherlock is kidnapped just before the plucky gumshoe is about to say, “I do.” It’s always fun to see Millie do her thing alongside her talented returning cast, including Henry Cavill, Helena Bonham Carter, Sharon Duncan-Brewster, Himesh Patel, and Louis Partridge, among others. — Adrienne

entries.name: Human Vapor
entries.description: Yeon Sang-ho, the creator of Train to Busan, has reimagined Ishirō Honda's 1960 sci-fi thriller as a TV series where a figure called the Human Vapor publicly announces he's the entity who is responsible for a series of killings across Japan. To cause further panic and chaos, he announces each target in advance. The show is the first partnership between Netflix and the truly legendary Japanese studio, Toho. — Arno

entries.name: Silo
entries.detail: Season 3 Streaming on on Apple TV
entries.description: “Silo,” one of your fave series (and ours), splits its timeline in half for Season 3. In the present, Juliette (Rebecca Ferguson) returns to her silo after her forced “cleaning” at the end of Season 2. While Juliette copes with memory loss, rebounding from the rebellion, and a new threat (of course), the storyline will travel back to the “Before Times,” where a journalist and Congressman uncover a conspiracy that leads up to the construction of ... well, you know. Season 3 premieres on July 3 and weekly episodes will drop through September 4. — Arno

entries.name: Descendants: Wicked Wonderland"
entries.description: For those keeping track, this is the fifth film in the mega-popular musical series about the children of classic Disney villains. In this installment, Red (Kylie Cantrell) and Chloe (Malia Baker) team up to stop a new villain, Maddox Hatter (Brendon Tremblay) after he captures  Red’s mother (the Queen of Hearts) and threatens all of Wonderland. — James

entries.name: Heartstopper Forever
entries.description: It’s time to bid Nick and Charlie adieu. After three seasons of romance, friendship, and growing up, the series from original graphic novel writer, Alice Oseman, is coming to an end with the film Heartstopper Forever. The Netflix original will follow our happy couple as they face the challenges of what it might mean to have a long distance relationship after high school. Is this the end of Charlie and Nick’s love story? Or, can true love find a way to last after the last bell rings? — Adrienne

entries.name: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe
entries.detail: Series premieres July 23 on HBO Max
entries.description: True to its name, the Big Bang franchise is expanding, and in the case of "Stuart Fails to Save the Universe," it's even jumping realities. This time the story zeroes in on comic book store owner Stuart Bloom, who after accidentally creating a new multiverse must track down a quantum-interference device before reality collapses for good. If the title is anything to go by, we're not so sure things will work out well. Expect some familiar faces too, as "Big Bang Theory" alums Joshua Malina, John Ross Bowie, Lauren Lapkus, Wil Wheaton, and Riki Lindhome are all set to return. — Vanessa

entries.name: The Dink
entries.description: Given the sport's popularity, it's surprising there aren't more pickleball movies out there, but producer Ben Stiller (Dodgeball) and director Josh Greenbaum (Barb and Star Go to Vista Del Mar) are here to change that. Jake Johnson stars as a washed-up tennis pro forced into pickleball after an injury, who reluctantly falls for the sport with the help of his new partner and guide (Mary Steenburgen). The all-star cast includes Patton Oswalt, Chloe Fineman, Chris Parnell, Aaron Chen, and Ed Harris. — Vanessa

entries.name: The Walking Dead: Dead City
entries.description: After two seasons of battling it out, Negan and Maggie are finally ... allies? Yep, but even with this hopeful premise, you can expect nonstop walker bashing, rival human factions, and even an episode set in an alternate reality as this odd couple attempts to build the first thriving community in Manhattan since the apocalypse. Jimmi Simpson, Aimee Garcia, Raúl Castillo, and Logan Kim join the cast for Season 3. — Vanessa

entries.name: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
entries.description: If you needed proof that Spider-Man: Brand New Day could be the biggest movie of the summer, it's already set the record for the biggest trailer launch ever. Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings and “Wonder Man” director Destin Daniel Cretton helms Spider-Man's return, picking up with Peter Parker now forgotten by his friends and fighting crime until a surprising physical evolution threatens his existence. Tom Holland, Zendaya, and Jacob Batalon return alongside MCU alumni Jon Bernthal and Mark Ruffalo, plus (hopefully) Sadie Sink. We're all really hoping those Jean Grey rumors are true!

entries.name: I Want Your Sex
entries.description: Director Greg Araki’s first feature in a decade is kind of a sex comedy, kind of a relationship drama about an indecent arrangement that becomes something more as feelings find their way into the mix. Olivia Wilde plays the successful Millennial hot girl ideal, complete with laissez-faire attitudes about sex and a heaping helping of generational judgment, while Cooper Hoffman tries to decide what he wants out of a relationship that already has major red flags since it’s with his literal boss. Be ready for bawdy scenes but also some fantastic acting as Hoffman and Wilde test the limits of love, consent, and art. — Adrienne

entries.name: Batman: Caped Crusader
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres July 31 on Prime Video
entries.description: This spiritual successor to “Batman: The Animated Series” was a huge success in its first season on Prime Video, and Season 2 promises a mix of heavy hitters (The Riddler and Joker) as well as re-vamped villains from the Dark Knight’s rogues gallery, like Roxy Rocket and a gender-swapped Mad Hatter. There are sure to be more surprise guests when alll 10 episodes drop on July 31. — James

plot_text.plain_text: A reformed criminal is forced to confront her past and return to illicit activities for one final job, hoping to secure her freedom and leave her former life behind for good.
Follows Lonnie Hawkins, 2004's top golfer, as he struggles to recapture his magic late in his career, refusing to believe he's anything other than one stroke away from golf's greatest comeback.
After the Trojan War, Odysseus faces a dangerous voyage back to Ithaca, meeting creatures like the Cyclops Polyphemus, Sirens, and Calypso along the way.

node.text: A Morte do Demônio: Em Chamas
La Casa: Il rogo del male

plot_text.plain_text: After the loss of her husband, a woman seeks solace with her in-laws. As one by one they transform into deadites, she comes to discover that the vows she took in life - survive even in death.

node.text: La Petite Maison dans la prairie

plot_text.plain_text: The Ingalls family lives and works on a farm in Midwestern America during the late 19th century.

node.text: Vaiana: La Légende du bout du monde

plot_text.plain_text: Live-action adaptation of the 2016 Disney animated film 'Moana.'
Follows the Minions in 1920s Hollywood as they search for frightening creatures for their monster movie, partner with a green creature, and must save the planet after unleashing monsters.
Follows Elle Woods' journey through high school, exploring the formative experiences that mold her into the iconic, confident "Legally Blonde" character.
A band of mutants use their uncanny gifts to protect a world that hates and fears them; they're challenged like never before, forced to face a dangerous and unexpected new future.
Detective Enola Holmes heads to Malta, where her aspirations merge in her most complex and dangerous case yet.
A man gains the ability to transform into a gaseous state uses his powers for bank robberies.
Men and women live in a giant silo underground with several regulations which they believe are in place to protect them from the toxic and ruined world on the surface.

node.text: Os Descendentes: Viagem ao Mundo Sombrio

plot_text.plain_text: The latest installment in the fantasy teen series. When Wonderland plays host to the Kingdom Cup Games, Red and Chloe Charming must fight to save everyone from an unexpected villain.
Nick and Charlie are in love, but new challenges lay ahead with Nick getting ready to leave for university and Charlie taking on new responsibilities at school.

plot_text.plain_text: Comic book store owner Stuart Bloom, from The Big Bang Theory (2007), returns in a new series where he attempts--and apparently fails--to save the universe, staying true to his unlucky nature.

original_title_text.text: Stuart Fails to Save the Universe

node.text: The Dink: Pasión por el pickleball

plot_text.plain_text: A washed-up tennis pro tries to save a club by reluctantly playing pickleball to earn his father's respect.
Maggie and Negan travel into a post-apocalyptic Manhattan long ago cut off from the mainland. The city is filled with the dead and denizens who have made New York City their own world.

node.text: スパイダーマン：ブランド・ニュー・デイ

plot_text.plain_text: A forgotten Peter Parker lives alone as a full-time Spider-Man until mounting pressure triggers a dangerous change and a powerful new enemy emerges.
Elliot lands a job for Erika Tracy as her sexual muse.

node.text: バットマン：マントの戦士

plot_text.plain_text: After a family tragedy, affluent philanthropist Bruce Wayne transforms into The Batman, where his crusade for justice spawns unforeseen ramifications.
